steps.title: Identify the key timing fact
steps.content: Grande Terre emerged 37 million years ago, but Sauquet et al. found the crown age of southern beeches on Grande Terre is 16.4 million years — so the clade's most recent common ancestor lived after the island emerged.

steps.title: Connect the timeline to the answer
steps.content: A clade whose most recent common ancestor lived 16.4 million years ago could not have existed when Grande Terre was emerging 37 million years ago. Choice C expresses this conclusion: the southern beeches are not members of a clade that existed before the island completely emerged. The other choices contradict the timing—A falsely claims origin outside the South Pacific (where crown age is identical), B falsely places the ancestor before the emergence—or lack supporting evidence (D). That matches choice C — C is correct.
